svg.text: Blinky W = 4 — EEPROM → fast-SRAM boot loader
8-bit counter copies all 256 bytes · 1 MHz oscillator · self-debouncing SR latch · WRPH-gated /WE · wrap-to-finish · single 8-bit EEPROM + single 8-bit SRAM · CPU left paused (no /RESET drive)

g.text: distributed signals shown as net-name labels
→ write pulse confined to OSC low half
CPU not reset by loader — leave it paused in single-step.
1. Press Load → SR latch sets; contact bounce ignored.
2. U2a re-clocks it on OSC → loading aligned to phase.
3. loading=1: /SR frees counter from 0; EEPROM drives bus,
SRAM /OE off, /WE pulses each cycle's settled half.
4. Each OSC tick: EEPROM[N] → SRAM[N], then N++.
5. N=255 → U4 RCO → U2b. Next tick wraps N→0 and U2b
clears the latch after byte 255's write closes.
6. loading=0: mux→PC, EEPROM Hi-Z, /WE off; CPU resumes.
